Decentralized identity
旅行の写真を整理中。景色12枚、食べ物47枚。まあ、そうなるよね。
読みたい本を買ったはずなのに、料理の本だけが増えていく。なぜ。
今日はプリン。以上です。
休日に店を3軒回ったら、最後はカフェで動けなくなりました。
ライブのチケット取った。帰りの電車はまだ決めてない。たぶん大丈夫。
今日のラーメン、スープが最後まで熱かった。大事。
毎日通る道に新しい花屋さんができてた。こういうのが一番うれしい。
連絡先に店の名前まで入れておく派です。あとで絶対わからなくなるから。
おみやげは小さな焼き菓子にしがち。持ち運びやすいし、すぐ食べられるので。
旅の予定は半分だけ決めて、あとは現地で考えるくらいが好きです。
読み終わったあと、しばらく何もしたくなくなる本が好きです。
祖母のレシピと同じ名前のお菓子を見つけた。今度つくる。
失敗パンです。見た目はすごいけど、味は普通でした。
旅行の荷物、減らしたい。でも折りたたみ傘は必要。毎回これ。
朝の散歩。知らない花、今日も増えてた。
雨。昔のアルバム。以上、今日はこれでいい。
読みかけの本を読み終わるまで新しい本は買わない、という決意をしました（買いました）。
窓際の席、空いてた。勝ち。
Give me one honest restaurant review. “You have to try this” tells me nothing.
One good kitchen tool beats a drawer full of gadgets. I will not say which tool because we all have different needs.
Took the weekend off. The code is still there. Amazing.
Road trip rule: stop when you see a bakery. The schedule will survive.
Weather forecast: shoes, umbrella, emotional preparation.
Coffee first. The routine can wait.
Over-packing is a problem until the temperature drops. Then it is called foresight.
Lunch leaderboard today: outdoor seating wins. No notes.
Searching for a song by “the one with the blue album cover” is a lifestyle, apparently.
Weather app says “clear skies.” My bag contains three rain jackets anyway.
Afternoon meeting survival kit: snacks. I have data.
Leave one afternoon blank on every trip. Trust me on this.
Picnic checklist: napkins, sunscreen, bottle opener. We forgot the bottle opener.
My friends know two things about me: I will order dessert, and I will ask for a bite of theirs.
Cafe tip: the quietest seat is usually farthest from the speakers. Also the best one.
Every apartment has one chair whose only job is holding clothes. You cannot convince me otherwise.
Tried to play guitar while assembling a shelf. The shelf won.
Breakfast opinion: toast is a meal, not a side dish.
Small communities are just people sending each other lunch recommendations and I support this.
"Just eyeball it," said the recipe. The cake is now structurally interesting.
My niece beat me at cards again. I am beginning to suspect this is not luck.
Note to self: write down the restaurant name before Saturday. Future me is not going to remember.
Keys were in the fridge. I have no further comments at this time.
Logging off to run before the rain. Mystery novel recs welcome.
Moved the reading lamp six inches and suddenly I can actually see the page. Huge day.
Picnic research: what is your undefeated summer snack? Mine is salt-and-vinegar chips.
Saving this for movie night. Also saving room for snacks.
That sourdough was absurdly good. I have been thinking about it for three days.
Tiny app update: grocery exports no longer buy six jars of mustard. Personal milestone.
Morning note to self: tea. More tea. Somehow I ran out again.
Day trip or vacation? The answer is in how much luggage you bring.
Who has not had coffee yet? This is important research.
Went for a five-minute walk before the first meeting. Still had the third coffee.
ok the cinnamon roll really IS that good
New desk lamp. Same desk. Weirdly feels like I got promoted.
The soup was supposed to last three days. It lasted one and a half. No regrets.
Planned the week on paper. Forgot the paper at home. Strong start.
Running reminder: check the weather. The sky is lying.
Museum, long walk, nap. I have solved the weekend.
Dumplings: 10/10. Kitchen afterwards: absolutely not.
The tomatoes are finally red 🍅 Also, the July meetup has a quiet room this time.
New coffee place by the station: tiny tables, great toast, no queue before 9. I will be back.
Draft: volunteer rota and contact numbers before I publish the event post.
